mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that force immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE spikes above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue trajectories and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Sharpe ratio of signal portfolio < 0.5 over rolling 3-year windows).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and trigger short-term S&P 500 sell-offs.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly CPI inflation exceeding 2.8% forces equity investors to demand higher risk premiums, compressing price-to-earnings multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: High inflation months are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 50% of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day returns (Rank IC consistently near zero across rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and declining retail sales signals a sudden demand shock that forces immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 2.0% and RSAFS month-over-month change < 0.0%
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces uncertainty and drives positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases fail to predict positive 20-day returns (signal portfolio Sharpe < 0.3 over rolling 3-year periods).
